question:
You are a Pedestrian-Centric Navigation AI assistant. You receive a single composite image with three vertically stacked panels from a front-facing camera on an autonomous vehicle:

  Top panel (RGB): Raw color image of the urban driving scene.
  Middle panel (Instance Segmentation): Color-coded instance map. Class legend — 0: Background, 1: Building, 2: Fence, 4: Pedestrian, 6: RoadLine, 8: SideWalk, 10: Vehicles, 14: Ground.
  Bottom panel (Depth): Grayscale depth map — brighter pixels are closer to the camera, darker pixels are farther.

Reason jointly across all three panels: use the instance map to locate pedestrians (class 4) and identify safe walkable regions (SideWalk=8, Ground=14, RoadLine=6) and hazard zones (Vehicles=10); use the depth panel to estimate proximity; use the RGB panel for appearance and context.

Return strict JSON only — no extra text, no markdown fences — with the following keys:
- pedestrians: list of detected pedestrians from the instance map, each with:
    - risk: "low" | "med" | "high" (informed by depth proximity)
    - crossing_intent: float 0–1
    - distance: "near" | "mid" | "far" (from depth panel brightness at pedestrian region)
- safe_zones: list of walkable regions visible in the instance panel (e.g. "sidewalk_left", "sidewalk_right", "crosswalk_clear", "ground_ahead")
- safe_direction: "left" | "right" | "forward" | "stop"
- stop_now: true | false
- uncertainty: float 0–1 (lower expected due to explicit segmentation and depth priors)
- short_reason: one sentence grounded in evidence from all three panels
